title: How a War Affects the UK's Money and Prices
summary: A war between the US, Israel, and Iran is making things harder for the UK's economy. A group called the OECD says the UK's growth, which is how much the country's money grows, will be only 0.7% this year, down from 1.2%. This means the UK is hurt the most among big countries. The war has caused oil and gas prices to go up because ships can't use an important route called the Strait of Hormuz and some plants are damaged. This makes petrol and heating more expensive for people in the UK. Also, inflation, which is how fast prices rise, is predicted to be 4% this year, higher than before. The UK government says they have a plan to protect families, but others worry the economy is weak. The war might also make food prices go up next year if fertilizer stays costly.

keywords.explanation: how much a country's money increases over time
when prices for things go up quickly
a group that studies how countries' economies are doing
a busy waterway where many oil ships travel
fuel used for cars, also called gasoline
the people who make rules and plans for a country

questions.question: What is the OECD's forecast for UK growth this year?
Why have oil and gas prices gone up due to the war?
options: Ships can't use the Strait of Hormuz
The UK is growing too fast
questions.correct_answer: Ships can't use the Strait of Hormuz

questions.question: What is inflation predicted to be in the UK this year?
Who said the UK has the right economic plan to protect families?
What might happen to food prices next year if fertilizer stays expensive?
Which country is hurt the most by the war among big economies?

background_read: The UK is part of a group of big economies called the G20. When there's a war, like between the US, Israel, and Iran, it can disrupt things like oil shipping from places like the Strait of Hormuz. This makes energy prices go up, which affects how much money the country makes (growth) and how fast prices rise (inflation). Groups like the OECD study these effects to help people understand what might happen.
Article_Structure: WHO: The OECD and UK government officials like Chancellor Rachel Reeves
WHAT: The UK's economic growth is slowing and inflation is rising due to the Iran war
WHERE: In the UK and globally, especially affecting the Strait of Hormuz
WHY: Because the war disrupts oil and gas supply, leading to higher prices and economic challenges

perspectives.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves says the UK has a plan to protect the economy from global problems.
Shadow chancellor Sir Mel Stride thinks the economy is weak because of the government's actions.
